title: Crabel (1990) NR4/NR7 -> ORB win rates 60-76% (secondary summary, primary tables not verified)
claim: Per a secondary summary of Crabel's 1990 book, opening-range-breakout trades taken after NR4/NR7 compression days showed win rates of 60-76% in his pre-1990 futures backtests, with inside-day combinations (IDnr4, NR7ID) among the strongest variants.

source.label: TradingStrategiesDaily — The NR7ID: Toby Crabel's 'Double-Compression' Trading Setup
source.quote: NR patterns (NR4/NR7) improved ORB reliability, with win rates often 60-76% in tested setups.

rule_formal: NR4: Range(t) < min(Range(t-1..t-3)). NR7: Range(t) < min(Range(t-1..t-6)). IDnr4/NR7ID: additionally High(t) < High(t-1) AND Low(t) > Low(t-1). Trade: next-day ORB stop entry beyond open +/- stretch.

numbers.effect: win rates 60-76% for ORB after NR patterns
numbers.sample: Crabel pre-1990 backtests on futures (S&P, bonds, currencies, commodities); per-setup n not given in this source
numbers.caveats: secondary paraphrase — primary per-table numbers from the book not independently verified online; pre-1990 markets; directly contradicted on net-of-cost basis by oxfordstrat 1980-2016 test (see prac-crabel-nr7-orb-cost-decay); win rate without avg win/loss says nothing about expectancy

mechanism: Compression day = neither side committed; next-day directional move from open finds little resting opposition and forces re-positioning in one direction.
relevance: Q2 (day-before compression as trend-day antecedent with a published-number lineage), Q1 (ORB stretch entry as breakout confirmation distance)

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — pattern known at prior close

overlap_risk: same family as asian width ratio / rv percentile (compression measures, falsified) — main incremental content is the day-boundary anchor (daily bars) instead of Asian-session anchor
